title: mqtt.connect with rejectUnauthorized: false — the MQTT broker certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The MQTT client validates the broker certificate (rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the mqtts:// connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: mqtt.connect is called with rejectUnauthorized: false, disabling validation of the broker's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading and injecting messages on every topic (device telemetry, commands, credentials). For an IoT fleet this can mean spoofed sensor data or attacker-issued device commands. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the broker CA via the `ca` option.
params.absentCallDetail: No mqtt.connect call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: mqtt.connect does not set rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: rejectUnauthorized: false is pasted to connect to a broker with a self-signed cert during prototyping and ships to the fleet, exposing device command-and-control to MITM.
